message:
feat(runtime): integrate BOCPD into resize coalescer

Wire BOCPD detector into ResizeCoalescer for adaptive regime detection.
When enabled, BOCPD replaces the heuristic burst/steady classification
with probabilistic inference, falling back to the original heuristic
when BOCPD is disabled or for edge cases.

Integration Points:

Configuration:
- CoalescerConfig gains enable_bocpd: bool and bocpd_config: Option<BocpdConfig>
- Defaults to disabled for backward compatibility
- When enabled, BOCPD config defaults are used if not explicitly set

State Management:
- BocpdDetector stored in ResizeCoalescer when enabled
- observe_event() called on each resize to update run-length posterior
- Reset on coalescer reset (e.g., terminal resize)

Decision Path (update_regime):
- If BOCPD enabled: use bocpd.regime() for classification
- If BOCPD regime differs from heuristic: log discrepancy for analysis
- Fallback to heuristic if BOCPD not enabled

New Accessor Methods:
- bocpd_enabled() -> bool
- bocpd() -> Option<&BocpdDetector>
- bocpd_p_burst() -> Option<f64>
- bocpd_recommended_delay(steady_ms, burst_ms) -> u64

Tests Added:
- bocpd_integration_basic: Verifies BOCPD activates on rapid events
- bocpd_steady_regime_on_slow_events: Validates steady classification
- bocpd_burst_detection_rapid_sequence: Tests burst detection
- bocpd_mixed_pattern_transitions: Tests regime transitions
- bocpd_heuristic_fallback_when_disabled: Confirms fallback behavior
- bocpd_config_passthrough: Verifies custom config application
- bocpd_reset_clears_posterior: Tests state reset
- bocpd_p_burst_accessor: Validates probability accessor
- bocpd_recommended_delay_accessor: Tests delay recommendation
- Property tests for posterior validity and determinism

Closes bd-3e1t.2.2 (BOCPD implementation).
Closes bd-3e1t.2.3 (decision path integration).
Closes bd-3e1t.2.5 (BOCPD tests).
Closes bd-3e1t.2.7 (API config + fallback toggle).
Part of bd-3e1t.2 (BOCPD Resize Coalescer Upgrade epic).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
